title: persea excelsa
intro: Persea excelsa, also known as the West Indian bay tree, is a species of evergreen tree in the family Lauraceae, native to Central and South America. The leaves and bark of this tree have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of P. excelsa include reducing fever and pain, as well as treating respiratory issues and digestive problems. The leaves and bark can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and are also used in cooking and perfumery due to their aromatic properties.
